revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:إنجيشيكي (كتاب ياباني)]][[de:Engishiki]][[en:Engishiki]][[es:Engishiki]][[fr:Engishiki]][[id:Engishiki]][[it:Engishiki]][[ja:延喜式]][[ko:엔기시키]][[min:Engishiki]][[mr:इंगिशिकी]][[nl:Engishiki]][[pt:Engishiki]][[ru:Энгисики]][[simple:Engishiki]][[tr:Engishiki]][[uk:Енґісікі]][[zh:延喜式]]
{{Short description|Japanese book about laws and customs}} {{italic title}} The {{Nihongo|'''''Engishiki'''''|延喜式||extra="Procedures of the Engi Era"}} is a {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}ese book about laws and customs. The major part of the writing was completed in 927.<ref name="nussbaum178">{{ill|Louis Frédéric|en|Louis Frédéric|qid=Q726040|lt=Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric|1=Louis Frédéric}}. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&pg=PA178&dq= "''Engi-shiki''"] in ''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 178.</ref> ==History== In 905, {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|qid=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo|1=Emperor Daigo}} ordered the compilation of the ''Engishiki''. Although previous attempts at codification are known to have taken place, neither the ''Konin'' nor the ''Jogan Gishiki''<ref>"Jogan Gishiki" in Stuart D. B. Pecken, ed., ''Historical Dictionary of Shinto''. Second edition. (Lanham, MD, USA: Scarecrow Press, Inc., 2011) p. 139.</ref> survive, making the Engishiki important for early Japanese historical and religious studies.<ref>" Engishiki" in Stuart D. B. Pecken, ed., ''Historical Dictionary of Shinto''. Second edition. (Lanham, MD, USA: Scarecrow Press, Inv, 2011) p. 92.</ref> {{ill|Fujiwara no Tokihira|en|Fujiwara no Tokihira|qid=Q2994735|lt=Fujiwara no Tokihira|1=Fujiwara no Tokihira}} began the task, but work stalled when he died four years later in 909. His brother {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadahira|en|Fujiwara no Tadahira|qid=Q1397601|lt=Fujiwara no Tadahira|1=Fujiwara no Tadahira}} continued the work in 912 eventually completing it in 927.<ref name="nussbaum178"/> After a number of revisions, the work was used as a basis for reform starting in 967. ==Contents== The text is 50 volumes in lengths and is organized by department: *volumes 1–10: [[Department of Divinities|Department of Worship]]: In addition to regulating ceremonials including [[Daijosai|Daijyō-sai]] (the first [[Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] following the accession of a new emperor) and worship at [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and [[Saikū]], this section of the ''Engishiki'' recorded liturgical texts, listed all 2,861 {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}s existing at the time, except for {{ill|Shikigesha|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigesha|qq=}} and {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|qq=}},<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8610 |access-date=2023-04-10 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref> and listed the 3,131 officially-recognized and enshrined [[Kami]].<ref>" Engishiki" in Stuart D. B. Pecken, ed., ''Historical Dictionary of Shinto''. Second edition. (Lanham, MD, USA: Scarecrow Press, Inc., 2011) p. 92.</ref> {{ill|Felicia Gressitt Bock|en|Felicia Gressitt Bock|qid=Q124734605|lt=Felicia Gressitt Bock|1=Felicia Gressitt Bock}} published a two-volume annotated English language translation with an introduction entitled ''Engi-shiki; procedures of the Engi Era'' in 1970. *volumes 11–40: [[Daijō-kan|Department of State]] and [[Daijō-kan#The Eight Ministries|Eight Ministries]] *volumes 41–49: Other departments *volume 50: Miscellaneous laws == Engishiki Jinmyōchō == Engishiki Jinmyōchō is a part of the Engishiki where the main {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} and [[kami|gods]] of Japan are listed.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sohsha.jp/common/img/honor.pdf|title=The History of Harima|website=sohsha.jp|access-date=28 April 2024}}</ref> It is from it that many categorizations of Shinto shrines are found * Myōjin Taisha (名神大社) ones listed as especially significant.<ref name=":0myojin" /><ref name=":1" /><ref name=":2" /><ref name=":3" /><ref name=":4" /><ref name=":5" /><ref name=":6" /> * {{nihongo|Shikinai Taisha|式内大社}} another class * Shikinai Shōsha (式內小社) minor shrines listed * Shikigesha (式外社) ones that were ignored by both the Engishiki Jinmyōchō and the [[Rikkokushi]] * Kokushi genzaisha (国史見在社) ones in the [[Rikkokushi]] but not the Engishiki.<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":7" /><ref name=":8" /><ref name=":9" /> === Myōjin Taisha === '''Myōjin Taisha''' is a high Rank of a Shinto shrine.<ref name=":0myojin" /><ref name=":1">{{Cite book |last=Watanabe |first=Yasutada |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=3efpAAAAMAAJ&q=myojin+taisha |title=Shinto Art: Ise and Izumo Shrines |date=1974 |publisher=Weatherhill/Heibonsha |isbn=978-0-8348-1018-1 |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=J2pRAAAAYAAJ&q=myojin+taisha |title=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies |date=2002 |publisher=International Institute for the Study of Religions |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":3">{{Cite book |last=Moerman |first=David Leo |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=rcpEAQAAIAAJ&q=myojin+taisha |title=Localizing Paradise: Kumano Pilgrimage in Medieval Japan |date=1999 |publisher=Stanford University |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":4">{{Cite book |last=Matsumoto |first=Yoshinosuke |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=agQRAQAAIAAJ&q=myojin+taisha |title=The Hotsuma Legends: Paths of the Ancestors |date=1999 |publisher=Japan Translation Centre |isbn=978-4-931326-01-9 |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":5">{{Cite book |last=Moerman |first=David Max |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=ikTZAAAAMAAJ&q=myojin+taisha |title=Localizing Paradise: Kumano Pilgrimage and the Religious Landscape of Premodern Japan |date=2005 |publisher=Harvard University Asia Center |isbn=978-0-674-01395-7 |language=en}}</ref><ref name=":6">{{Cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=gfgQAQAAMAAJ&q=myojin+taisha |title=国立歴史民俗博物館硏究報告 |date=2008 |publisher=国立歴史民俗博物館 |language=ja}}</ref> These shrines are considered "great shrines" or "taisha" under the ancient system of shrine Rankings. Myōjin Taisha shrines are found throughout Japan, particularly in the Kyoto-Osaka region, including Yamashiro, Yamato, Ōmi, Mutsu, Tajima, and Kii provinces. There are 224 shrines that enshrine 310 kami listed as Myōjin Taisha in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Additionally, there are 203 shrines with 285 kami listed for Myojinsai or "festivals for famed deities" in book 3 of Engishiki. While most of the shrines in these two listings overlap, there are some differences in names and numbers. There are several theories about these differences, but it is unclear why the lists differ.<ref name=":0myojin">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/?id=9685|access-date=2023-04-10|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref> Myōjin Taisha is one of the highest Ranks of Shinto shrines.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Original History - Ooasahiko Jinja |url=https://www.ooasahikojinja.jp/e/yuisho/ |access-date=2023-10-13 |website=www.ooasahikojinja.jp}}</ref> A related list is the Kokushi genzaisha (国史見在社) which refers to shrines which appear in the Rikkokushi (六国史) but not in the Engishiki<ref>{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細|url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=8610|access-date=2023-04-10|website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム|language=ja}}</ref> * === Shikinai Taisha === Shikinai Taisha (式内大社) are shrines that are listed in volumes 9 and 10 of the "Engishiki" as Shinto shrines, also known as Shikinaisha, that are Ranked as major shrines. There are 492 of these shrines listed. This Category includes both the historical shrines and their modern equivalents. However, shrines that are designated as "{{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|simple|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}}" are not included in this Category. === Shikinai Shōsha === Shikinai Shōsha (式內小社) are shrines listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō as minor shrines. === Shikigesha === Shikigesha (式外社) refers to Shinto shrines that were known to have existed in the early 10th century when the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|simple|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} was being written, but were not included in it. Shikigesha, therefore, were considered "off-register" or "unofficial" shrines that were not recognized by the government as official state shrines. Shikigesha can be further classified into various categories, including shrines outside the control of the imperial court, those with their own power and influence, shrines that integrated Buddhism into their practices, and shrines managed by Buddhist monks. Additionally, some Shikigesha lacked proper formal shrine buildings. Shikigesha contrast with Shikinaisha which are shrines that were recorded in the Engishiki. Kokushi genzaisha are a type of Shikigesha which appear in the [[Rikkokushi]].<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":7" /><ref name=":8" /><ref name=":9" /> === Kokushi genzaisha === Kokushi genzaisha (国史見在社) are a type of Shinto shrine. It means a shrine that appears in the [[Rikkokushi]] (六国史) but not in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]<ref name=":0" /><ref name=":7">{{cite web|title=「It,is,Kokushi,genzaisha,shrines,not,listed,in,the,registers,but,mentioned,Six,National,Histories」を使った英語表現・例文・フレーズ｜Cheer up! English |url=http://english.cheerup.jp/corpus/search?name=It+is+Kokushi+genzaisha+shrines+not+listed+in+the+registers+but+mentioned+Six+National+Histories |access-date=2023-10-16 |website=Cheer up! English |language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":8">{{cite web |title=国史見 - Translation into English - examples Japanese {{!}} Reverso Context |url=https://context.reverso.net/translation/japanese-english/%E5%9B%BD%E5%8F%B2%E8%A6%8B |access-date=2023-10-16 |website=context.reverso.net}}</ref><ref name=":9">{{cite journal |last=Grapard |first=Allan G. |date=2002 |title=Shrines Registered in Ancient Japanese Law: Shinto or Not? |url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/30233722 |journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies |volume=29 |issue=3/4 |pages=209–232 |jstor=30233722 |issn=0304-1042}}</ref> The [[Rikkokushi]] or the Six Official Histories, includes [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon shoki]], [[Shoku Nihongi|Shoku nihongi]], [[:en:Nihon kōki|Nihon kōki]], [[Shoku Nihon Kōki|Shoku nihon kōki]], [[Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Montoku jitsuroku]], and [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Sandai jitsuroku]]. They chronicle the mythology and history of Japan from the earliest times to 887.<ref>{{Cite book |title=Kodansha Encyclopedia of Japan |publisher=Kōdansha |year=1983 |editor=Kōdansha |id=}}</ref> The six histories were written at the imperial court during the 8th and 9th centuries, under order of the Emperors.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Sakamoto |first=Tarō |title=The Six National Histories of Japan |author2=tr. John S. Brownlee |publisher=UBC Press, University of Tokyo Press |year=1991 |isbn=0-7748-0379-7}}</ref> Kokushi gensaisha are also called kokushi shozaisha or "shrines that appear in the Official Histories". This gives them a high level of historical significance. Some of the shrines listed in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]] as Myōjin Taisha also overlap with the kokushi genzaisha, but the term usually refers to shrines that are only mentioned in the Official Histories.<ref name=":0" /> 国史 (Kokushi) means official history, 見在 gensai means appearing and 社 sha means shrine == Shrine lists == These are non-exhaustive lists of shrines of the given categories defined by the Engishiki === List of Myōjin Taisha === *[[Aso Shrine]] *[[:en:Atsuta Jingu|Atsuta Jingu]] *[[Awa Shrine]] *[[Awaga Shrine]] *[[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine]] *{{ill|Dewa Shrine|en|Dewa Shrine|qid=Q11395802|lt=Dewa Shrine|1=Dewa Shrine}} *[[Fujisan Hongū Sengen Taisha]] *[[Fushimi Inari-taisha]] *[[Futarasan shrine]] *[[Gassan Shrine]] *[[Hakozaki Shrine]] *[[Hikawa Shrine (Saitama)]] *[[Hinokuma Shrine]] *[[Hirano Shrine]] *[[Hiraoka Shrine]] *[[Hirose Taisha]] *[[Hirota Shrine]] *{{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|qid=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} *{{ill|Hotaka Shrine|en|Hotaka Shrine|qid=Q11597109|lt=Hotaka Shrine|1=Hotaka Shrine}} *{{ill|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|en|Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|qid=Q11557476|lt=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)|1=Ichinomiya Asama Shrine (Fuefuki)}} *{{ill|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|en|Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|qid=Q11352589|lt=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine|1=Ichinomiya Sengen Shrine}} *{{ill|Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)|en|Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)|qid=Q11352598|lt=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)|1=Ichinomiya Shrine (Tokushima)}} *{{ill|Ikonohime no Mikoto Shrine|simple|Ikonohime no Mikoto Shrine|ja|伊豫豆比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikonohime no Mikoto Shrine|1=Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11380620}} *[[Ikukunitama Shrine]] *[[Ikushimatarushima Shrine]] *[[Ikuta Shrine]] *[[Imizu Shrine]] *{{ill|Inbe Shrine|en|Inbe Shrine|qid=Q11490722|lt=Inbe Shrine|1=Inbe Shrine}} *[[Isasumi Shrine]] *[[Isono Shrine]] *[[Isonokami Jingu|Isonokami Shrine]] *[[Itakiso shrine]] *[[Itsukushima Shrine]] *[[Iwa Shrine]] *[[Izanagi Jingu|Izanagi Shrine]] *[[Izumo-daijingū]] *[[Izumo-taisha]] *[[Izushi Shrine]] *{{ill|Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|simple|Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|ja|伊豫豆比古命神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyozuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine|qq=}} *[[Kaijin Shrine]] *[[Kamado Shrine]] *[[Kamigamo Shrine]] *[[Kanasana Shrine]] *[[Kashima Jingū|Kashima Shrine]] *[[Kasuga-taisha]] *[[Katori Jingū|Katori Shrine]] *[[Kehi Jingū|Kehi Shrine]] *[[Keta Jinja]] *[[Keta Taisha]] *[[Kibitsu Shrine (Bitchū)]] *[[Kibitsuhiko Shrine]] *[[Kifune Shrine]] *[[:en:Kinpu Shrine (Yoshino)|Kinpu Shrine (Yoshino)]] *[[Kono Shrine]] *[[Kōra taisha]] *[[Kumano Hongū Taisha]] *[[Kumano Taisha]] *[[Masumida Shrine]] *[[Matsunoo Taisha]] *[[Mikami Shrine]] *[[Mishima Taisha]] *[[Munakata Taisha]] *[[Nagata Shrine]] *[[Nakayama Shrine]] *[[Nangū Taisha]] *[[Niukawakami Shrine]] *[[Niutsuhime Shrine]] *[[Nukisaki Shrine]] *{{ill|Ōasahiko Shrine|en|Ōasahiko Shrine|qid=Q723417|lt=Ōasahiko Shrine|1=Ōasahiko Shrine}} *[[Aotsuka Kofun|Oarai Shrine]] *[[Oarai Isosaki Shrine]] *{{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|qid=Q705542|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} *{{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya|en|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya|qid=Q546197|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya|1=Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya}} *{{ill|Onji Shrine|ja|恩智神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onji Shrine|qq=}} *[[Ōtori taisha]] *[[Ōyamato Shrine]] *[[Ōyamazumi Shrine]] *[[Sakatsura Isosaki Shrine]] * * *[[Samukawa Shrine]] *[[Shikaumi Shrine]] *[[Shimogamo Shrine]] *[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Fukuoka)]] *[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Shimonoseki)]] *[[Sumiyoshi-taisha]] *[[Suwa-taisha]] *[[Sumiyoshi Shrine (Iki City)]] *{{ill|Takemizuwake Shrine|simple|Takemizuwake Shrine|ja|武水別神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemizuwake Shrine|qq=}} *[[Tagata Shrine]] *{{ill|Take Shrine|en|Take Shrine|qid=Q11430665|lt=Take Shrine|1=Take Shrine}} *[[Tashima Shrine (Saga)|Tashima Shrine]] *[[Tado Taisha]] *[[Takebe taisha]] *[[Tamasaki Shrine]] *[[Tamura Shrine]] *[[Tatsuta Taisha]] *{{ill|Three Mountains of Dewa|en|Three Mountains of Dewa|qid=Q1155726|lt=Three Mountains of Dewa|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} *[[Tsukiyomi Shrine (Kyoto)]] *[[Tsukubasan Shrine]] *[[Tsutsukowake Shrine]] *[[Ube Shrine]] *[[Umenomiya Taisha]] *[[Usa Jingū]] *[[Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *[[Wakasahiko Shrine]] *[[Watatsumi Shrine (Kobe)|Watatsumi Shrine]] *[[Yahiko Shrine]] *{{ill|Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)|lt=Yamato Okunitama Shrine|simple|Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)|lt=Ōkunitama Shrine (disambiguation)|lt=Yamato Okunitama Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Okunitama Shrine|qq=}} === List of Shikinai Taisha === *[[Aekuni Shrine]] *[[Ikasuri Shrine]] *[[Ikoma Shrine]] *{{ill|Iwazononiimasu Takumushitama Shrine|ja|石園座多久虫玉神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwazononiimasu Takumushitama Shrine|qq=}} *[[Izawa-jinja]] *[[Izawa-no-miya]] *[[Kagoshima Jingū|Kagoshima Shrine]] *[[Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] *[[Sasamuta Shrine]] *{{ill|Shikiagatanushi Shrine|ja|志貴県主神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikiagatanushi Shrine|qq=}} *[[Susaki Shrine]] *[[Tosa Shrine]] *[[Uda Mikumari Shrine]] *{{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|en|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|qid=Q3529714|lt=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|1=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine}} === List of Shikinai Shōsha === * [[Atago Shrine (Kyoto)|Atago Shrine]] * [[Chichibu Shrine]] * {{ill|Chiryu Shrine|ja|知立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiryu Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Dewa Shrine|en|Dewa Shrine|qid=Q11395802|lt=Dewa Shrine|1=Dewa Shrine}} * {{ill|Fuji Rokusho Sengen Shrine|ja|富知六所淺間神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuji Rokusho Sengen Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 富知六所浅間神社}} * [[Hirasaki Shrine]] * [[Iminomiya Shrine]] * [[Itatehyōzu Shrine]] * [[Izusan Shrine]] * [[Kamayama Shrine]] * [[Komagata Shrine]] * [[Miho Shrine]] * [[Minashi Shrine]] * {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|en|Mononobe Shrine|qid=Q11570699|lt=Mononobe Shrine|1=Mononobe Shrine (Oda)}} * {{ill|Nunakuma Shrine|en|Nunakuma Shrine|qid=Q11554446|lt=Nunakuma Shrine|1=Nunakuma Shrine}} * [[Ōgamiyama Shrine]] * [[Okuni Shrine|Oguni shrine]] * {{ill|Onominato Shrine|ja|大野湊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onominato Shrine|qq=}} * [[Owari Ōkunitama Shrine]] * {{ill|Oyama Aburi Shrine|ja|大山阿夫利神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyama Aburi Shrine|1=Ōyama Afuri Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434713}} * [[Oyama Shrine (Tateyama)|Oyama Shrine]] * [[Sada Shrine]] * [[Shirayama Hime Shrine]] * [[Shitori Shrine (Tottori)|Shitori Shrine]] * [[Shizuoka Sengen Shrine]] * {{ill|Sugōisobe Shrine|lt=Sugōisobe Shrine|lt=Sugo Ishibe Shrine|ja|菅生石部神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugo Ishibe Shrine|qq=}} * [[Susa Shrine]] * [[Taga-taisha]] * [[Takase Shrine]] * {{ill|Tamanooya Shrine|en|Tamanooya Shrine|qid=Q11572731|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} * [[Toga Shrine]] * [[Tsukubasan Shrine]] * {{ill|Tsurugi Shrine|ja|劔神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurugi Shrine|qq=}} * [[Tsuno Shrine]] * [[Watatsu Shrine]] * [[Yaegaki Shrine]] === List of Shikigesha === *{{ill|Homori Shrine|ja|火守神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homori Shrine|1=Hinomori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22120305}} *{{ill|Iina Shrine|ja|飯名神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iina Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamoshita Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshita Shrine|1=Kamoshimo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17226760}} * {{ill|Karasu Shrine|ja|香良洲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasu Shrine|1=Karasu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11667717}} * {{ill|Shiogama shrine|en|Shiogama shrine|qid=Q133753|lt=Shiogama shrine|1=Shiogama shrine}} * {{ill|Taki Matsuri Shrine|ja|滝祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taki Matsuri Shrine|1=Takimatsuri-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17229063}} * {{ill|Wakiko Hachimangu Shrine|ja|脇子八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakiko Hachimangu Shrine|1=Wakiko-hachimangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11612128}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine|en|Yoshida Shrine|qid=Q713177|lt=Yoshida Shrine|1=Yoshida Shrine}} === List of Kokushi Gensaizha === *[[Akihasan Hongū Akiha Shrine]] *[[Hiromine Shrine]] *{{ill|Inaba Shrine|en|Inaba Shrine|qid=Q701414|lt=Inaba Shrine|1=Inaba Shrine}} *[[Iwashimizu Hachimangū]] *{{ill|Kaneda Castle|en|Kaneda Castle|qid=Q11647684|lt=Kaneda Castle|1=Kaneda Castle}} *[[Kashii-gū]] *[[Keta Wakamiya Shrine]] *{{ill|Kinowa Shrine|ja|城輪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinowa Shrine|qq=}} *[[Kitano Tenmangū]] *[[Kogane Shrine]] *{{ill|Koshiō Shrine|en|Koshiō Shrine|qid=Q11410984|lt=Koshiō Shrine|1=Koshiō Shrine}} *[[Kumano Nachi Taisha]] *{{ill|Kushifuru Shrine|en|Kushifuru Shrine|qid=Q3200650|lt=Kushifuru Shrine|1=Kushifuru Shrine}} *{{ill|Ōharano Shrine|en|Ōharano Shrine|qid=Q3594020|lt=Ōharano Shrine|1=Ōharano Shrine}} *{{ill|Oi Shrine|ja|%E5%A4%A7%E4%BA%95%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E5%B3%B6%E7%94%B0%E5%B8%82)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oi Shrine|qq=}} *{{ill|Omura Shrine|en|Omura Shrine|qid=Q63471|lt=Omura Shrine|1=Omura Shrine}} *[[Shirahige Shrine]] *[[Takachiho Shrine]] *{{ill|Tamukeyama Hachimangū|en|Tamukeyama Hachimangū|qid=Q11496939|lt=Tamukeyama Hachimangū|1=Tamukeyama Hachimangū}} *{{ill|Tenson Shrine|en|Tenson Shrine|qid=Q3082076|lt=Tenson Shrine|1=Tenson Shrine}} *[[Yasaka Shrine]] ==See also== * {{ill|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|en|Japanese Historical Text Initiative|qid=Q16973361|lt=Japanese Historical Text Initiative|1=Japanese Historical Text Initiative}} *''[[Ruijū Kokushi]]'', a categorized and chronological history text of the ''Six National Histories'' *{{ill|Historiography of Japan|en|Historiography of Japan|qid=Q5774518|lt=Historiography of Japan|1=Historiography of Japan}} *[[Beppyo Shrine]] * [[Ichinomiya]] * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Kanpei-taisha]] * [[Taisha (shrine)|Taisha]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==Further reading== * {{NKBJ}} * {{NKBD}} Library|qid=Q7971387|lt=Waseda University Library|1=Waseda University Library}}: volumes [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wa03/wa03_01594/ 1-50] and [http://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/wa03/wa03_01350/ 8-10] {{Shinto Shrines}}
